characters.name: Alexander
characters.description: AlexanderAffiliationMoDean'sRelativesJoint Boy(second cousin); unnamed brotherPhysical descriptionGenderMaleEyesBrownHairBrownPortrayalActorJeff McEneryFirst appearanceAin't No Reason to Get ExcitedAlexanderis a recurring character onLetterkenny, played by Canadian comedian and actorJeff McEnery. This must be where the dicks hang out, eh?— Alexander Alexander's second cousin,Joint Boy, describes him as "slow-learnin'." He is first encountered at the urinal in the men's washroom atMoDean's;WayneandMcMurrayenter and proceed to urinals on either side of him. Alexander's attempts to make conversation about Wayne's ex's new beau are not well-received, both on account of the topic and the location.

characters.name: Aly
characters.description: AlyAlso known asAly-catAffiliationThe Club, The GymPhysical descriptionGenderfemalePortrayalActorSash StrigaFirst appearanceWe Don't Fight at WeddingsLast appearanceOver and OutAlyis a recurring character onLetterkenny, played by Canadian actress Sash Striga. AfterSeason 6, she is almost always paired with her friendBianca. Aly is a guest atRonandDax's wedding, whereReillyandJonesyattempt to flirt with her and another friend (played by Taveeta Szymanowicz) from the city. The boys have "bruced up" their look to seem softer, but they are interested in more traditionally masculine men, and their attempts to backpedal and assert themselves as such backfire. In any case, they are not that interested in hockey players, describing themselves as "lacrosstitutes"(We Don't Fight at Weddings). A glimpse of her is next seen a season later at theClubwhereStewart,Roald, andGaehave been working (and selling green-dyed GHB, "for vigilante purposes"). This is also the first time she is seen accompanying Bianca, although neither character is yet named. When they find their drinks adulterated, they complain to theDealerfor failing to watch them and for selling his undyed GHB, saying everyone will only buy fromthe Skidsfrom now on—earning the Skids his enmity(What Could Be So Urgent?); he leaves them threatening and poorly proofread notes(Bush Party Season).

characters.name: Angie
characters.description: AngieRelativesWayne (ex-boyfriend)Biographical informationMarital statusSinglePhysical descriptionGenderFemaleEyesBrownHairBlondePortrayalActorKalinka PetrieFirst appearanceRelationshipsLast appearanceScorched EarthAngieis a recurring character onLetterkenny, andWayne's ex-girlfriend from prior to the start of the show. While referenced throughout the run of the show, she appears only in Seasons 2-4, and not again until Season 9. She is played by Kalinka Petrie. At the start of the series, Angie and Wayne have just broken up after five years of dating, starting in high school. It is also revealed that she cheated on him. It's implied she did not like fighting, leading Wayne to stop fighting. Wayne resumed his fighting career after their breakup as a result of Angie's new beau's boorish behavior(Ain't No Reason to Get Excited). She was working as a bartender atMoDean's(Wingman Wayne), but left town for a time after her and Wayne's breakup(Puck Bunny). She returned after Wayne's matchmaker said she would be "just right" for him(Relationships). While nothing came of it initially, she came to want Wayne again, arriving at thefarmjust asGail's cousinRosiearrived with a dog to breed with Stormy, Wayne's German shepherd(Finding Stormy a Stud).

characters.name: Anik
characters.description: Anik ArchambaultAlso known asAnikAffiliationLes HiquesRelativesJean-Guy (brother)Jean-Claude (ex-fiancé)Biographical informationMarital statusSinglePhysical descriptionGenderFemaleEyesGreenHairDark blondePortrayalActorKim CloutierFirst appearanceLes HiquesLast appearanceDegensAnikis a recurring character onLetterkenny, portrayed by Kim Cloutier. She first appears in Season 3, then throughout Seasons 5-10. She is from near the Ontario-Quebec border, and was in a relationship withDarylfor a brief time. Anik returns inShoresyas host of aBroDude-sponsored sports panel show,Questionable Call. Anik is one ofthe Hicks' Quebecois doppelgangers, the group which also includes Jean-Guy (Sébastien Huberdeau), Jean-Carl (Alexandre Landry), and Jean-Pierre (Domenic Di Rosa). She and Jean-Guy are brother and sister, and were raised in Hawkesbury(Bock et Biche). She first arrives as the three French men are fishing at the ice hole, interrupting an exchange of insults they have begun withDarylandDan. Daryl is taken by her beauty, but never works up the courage to speak to her, as he assumes she will not be able to understand him and that he is not attractive enough to rely on his looks alone. Anik waves to him as the French group departs, and he laments that she is gone forever(Les Hiques).

characters.name: Mennonites
characters.description: MennonitesAnita and Noah DyckAlso known asMenno-Nappers, Schmellies (derogatory)PortrayalFirst appearanceBush Party SeasonMennonitesare a familiar sight inLetterkenny, particularly members of theDyck family(pronounced"DIK"): The Dyck family represents a popular characterization of the Old Order Mennonites, who have a sizeable presence in southwestern Ontario. They are a conservative branch of the largerChristian Anabaptisttradition which follows the teachings of pacifist reformer Menno Simons. There is considerable diversity among the various Mennonite groups, but in broad terms there is a belief in separation from the world, simplicity of lifestyle, and communal salvation. This is seen in the Dycks' traditional garb, plain speech, and forgoing of modern conveniences like the automobile or power tools. It is also evident in Noah and Anita's ignorance of slang and pop culture, leading to multiple unintentional double entendres that embarrass their conversation partners in the secular world. Daryl, does my daughters' warm sticky pie not appeal to you…? Well then let us enjoy them on this day, as a community, with our mouths.— Noah

characters.name: Axe
characters.description: AxeAffiliationThe NativesPhysical descriptionGendermaleEyesbrownHairblackPortrayalActorBradley TrudeauFirst appearanceRaveLast appearanceOver and OutAxeis a recurring character onLetterkenny, played by Canadian actor Bradley Trudeau. He andSlashare almost exclusively seen accompanying "auntie"Tanis. Axe and Slash say little in earlier seasons, mostly seen escorting Tanis at her various encounters with the Letterkenny townfolk. The arrival of Shyla andShaniaonthe Rezupends this dynamic, however, to Tanis' scorn. They stole Axe and Slash from me. And I'm pretty sure they're banging them, 'cause they're fucking banging everyone. Skanks! … I really miss Axe and Slash. I'm disappointed in them. They're, like, my buddies, you know.— Tanis

characters.name: Bianca
characters.description: BiancaAlso known asBianca-donkAffiliationThe Club, The GymPhysical descriptionGenderfemalePortrayalActorNadine BhabhaFirst appearanceWhat Could Be So Urgent?Last appearanceOver and OutBiancais a recurring character onLetterkenny, played by Canadian actress Nadine Bhabha. She is almost always paired with her friendAly. Bianca is first seen, along with Aly, at theClubwhereStewart,Roald, andGaehave been working (and selling green-dyed GHB, "for vigilante purposes"). When they find their drinks adulterated, they complain to theDealerfor failing to watch them and for selling his undyed GHB, saying everyone will only buy fromthe Skidsfrom now on—earning the Skids his enmity(What Could Be So Urgent?); he leaves them threatening and poorly proofread notes(Bush Party Season). Aly and Bianca come to Gae's hosue to warn them about the Dealer. Meanwhile, a panicked Roald returns toLetterkennyto seek help fromthe Hicks.Katyguilts the other Hicks into making the long drive to the City to help. When they arrive at the Club, however, their presences is found to be unnecessary—Aly, Bianca, and the club girls take on the Dealer and his muscle themselves, driving them out(Bush Party Season).

characters.name: Bradley
characters.description:
BradleyAffiliationMoDean'sRelativesRosie(favorite cousin),Gail(second favorite cousin)Physical descriptionGenderMaleEyesBrownHairBlackPortrayalActorAdrian HolmesFirst appearanceBradley Is a KillerBradleyis a guest character onLetterkennyinSeasons 3and4, played by Adrian Holmes. AfterMoDean'sII is trashed bydegens,Gailhires her cousin Bradley as a bouncer.The Hickslove Bradley and are enthralled by the news, and Bradley andRosieare each other's favorite cousin. They are gathered at MoDean's when Bradley arrives. He greets them warmly and does impressions of celebrities for them, making them howl with laughter. He also tells them that they are off degen duty, and are free to get as drunk as possible—againstKaty's strong advice.
Bradley successfully fends off the degens, but the Hicks become extremely drunk and extremely annoying.Darylpushes his nipples against him,Dancontinuously challenges him to foot races, andWayneconstantly asks him to do impressions, responding with his annoying laugh.

characters.name: Cassie
characters.description: CassieAffiliationBroDudePhysical descriptionGenderfemaleEyesbrownHairblondePortrayalActorLauren BurchFirst appearanceThe RippersLast appearanceInfluenzasCassieis a recurring character onLetterkennyin seasons 8–11, played by Canadian influencer and model Lauren Burch. She always appears with her brunette counterpartTassie(played by Lauren Burch's twin sister, Julia Burch). Cassie and Tassie are first seen accompanying theBroDuderep,Emma, and a photographer toMoDean'safter theKerry County Eagleswin the National Senior Hockey Championship(The Rippers). They have a mutual attraction withReillyandJonesy; after the hockey players' proposal for a men-only "BroDude Club Ferda" is rejected, they are instructed to arrange a sexy photo shoot. The boys do not believe including a girl only to sexualize them is PC, but ask if Cassie and Tassie might participate. Emma declares Neither one of these girls would cross the street to piss on you if you were on fire.— Emma

characters.name: Connor
characters.description: Connoris a nonspeaking member ofthe Skids. The tallest member of the Skids, he is regularly seen wearing a gas mask. He is played by Patrick McNeil.

characters.name: Dalton Thibodeaux
characters.description: Dalton Thibodeau is an unseenDegenwho is mentioned/heard in several episodes ofLetterkenny. Thibodeau figures prominently in the opening and closing scenes ofRed Card Yellow Cardas an employee of Keith "Gray Daniels" Greydahnus. Gray Daniels calls in to "Crack an Ag" to complain about a "tit" working at his sawmill; while he is reluctant to tell tails out of school,Wayneand the others get him to confirm it is Thibodeau, noting that "his old man's a fuckin' tit, too."Darynotes that he saw Thibodeau chain up a sick cow and drag it across his lawn because it was too sick to stand, prompting Wayne to retort in disgust, "Fuck you, Thibodeau." Gray Daniels, after some prodding, explains (in excruciating detail) that Thibodeau is slowing down production at the sawmill through his slower work process. Wayne and the others encourage Gray Daniels to "put a size nine in him," overriding Gray Daniels' concerns about offending "the kids today" because Thibodeau is "working fucking labor." They believe Gray Daniels should not give up on Thibodeau, but "keep your foot in it."  Gray Daniels appreciates the advice, but the very next caller is Thibodeau, who complains about Gray Daniels "telling tales out of school." This prompts the guys to all scream, "FUCK YOU, THIBODEAU!" and berate him until he hangs up.

characters.name: Dan
characters.description: DanielS10S9S8S7S6S5S4S3S2S1Also known asDanielSquirrely DanAffiliationHicksRelativesAt least four cousins; Garrett, Jarrett, Samuel and unnamed Niece.Biographical informationMarital statusSinglePhysical descriptionGenderMaleEyesHazelHairBrownPortrayalActorK. Trevor WilsonFirst appearanceAin't No Reason to Get ExcitedDan, nicknamedSquirrely Danby his close friends, is a main character onLetterkenny. He is played byK. Trevor Wilson. One of the coreHicks, Dan spends much of his time with Wayne and Katyat their farmalong withDaryl. He was apparently not childhood friends with them, however, and must have the history of Daryl's super soft birthdays explained to him(Super Soft Birthday). Dan is known for his long-winded stories, his large extended family, his oversharing of sexual details, and his flatulence. He also has an unusual speech pattern, pluralizing singular words by inserting an "S" at the end of seemingly random words.

characters.name: Daryl
characters.description: DarylS10S9S8S7S6S5S4S3S2S1Also known asDary (among close friends)AffiliationHicksBiographical informationMarital statusSinglePhysical descriptionGenderMaleEyesBlueHairDirty blondPortrayalActorNathan DalesFirst appearanceAin't No Reason to Get ExcitedDaryl, often calledDarryby his close friends, is one of the main characters onLetterkenny. He is played byNathan Dales, in a continuation of the persona developed forLetterkenny Problems. Daryl is a childhood friend ofWayneandKaty, and often spends time with them andSquirrely Danat thefarmand their other haunts in Letterkenny and beyond. Daryl is socially unrefined in some ways. He licks his lips after making a snot rocket, to Wayne's disgust(MoDeans 2), and is also known to wear his barn cothes toMoDean's(Ain't No Reason to Get Excited). He drives a decrepit van that Wayne is dismissive of(Fartbook). He is relatively taciturn, and speaks in a monotone, and mispronounces words likebasket. Nevertheless, he is shown to be quick-witted and intellectually curious, with a large vocabulary he can hurl at verbal opponents.

characters.name: Dax
characters.description: DaxAlso known asDaxyAffiliationGym ratRelativesRon(husband)Biographical informationMarital statusMarriedPhysical descriptionGenderMaleEyesBlueHairBrownPortrayalActorGregory WatersFirst appearanceNever Work a Day in Your LifeLast appearanceOver and OutDax(nicknamedDaxy) is a character onLetterkenny, portrayed byGregory Waters. He is the gym buddy and later husband ofRon, and is never seen independently of him. Dax was a minor recurring character inSeasons 4and5, but became a regular inSeason 6. Dax and Ron are first encountered byJonesy, when he is is working out alone at thegym. Dax and Ron approach him at the bench press and begin tossing hockey-related sexual innuendos at him.Reilly, working out alone later, receives the same kind of greeting. Dax: Who brought the fucking rocket, boys?Ron: Whose billet brother's a fucking rocket, boys?Dax: Let's play a little two-on-onesky.Ron: I'll play left, he'll play right, you fill the middle.Dax: Ride the pine, we'll change on-the-fly.Ron: Let's find your neutral zones.Dax: I'd love to backcheck you, bud.Ron: It's a butt-ending clinic over here, boys.Dax: You make my stick hot. Get it?Ron: Come penetrate the slot, mister.Dax: You a good D-man?Ron: I'll show you a good D, man.Dax: Why don't you go ahead and lay me some of that skin, Ronnie?Ron: Coming right up, Daxy.Reilly: I miss my buddy.Dax: I'll be your buddy.Ron: I'll be your buddy.Dax: What are we even doing here?

characters.name: Degens
characters.description:
The DegensPortrayalFirst appearanceSuper Soft Birthday:For the Season 11 episode titled "Degens," seeDegens (episode).
TheDegens from Upcountry(short for "degenerates") are an unorganized group of louts who form the Hicks' primary antagonist onLetterkennythrough Season 4. One or more of their number scrap with the Hicks on numerous occasions. The termsdegenordegenerateare used to label anyone guilty of bad behavior, on various occasions. Katy is the first to use the term, of various individuals coming to thefarmto challengeWayne's title as Toughest Guy in Letterkenny(Super Soft Birthday): Look, keep it at the end of the laneway. No degens on the property.— Katy The Hicks ascribe all manner of bad behavior to the degens, and resent that they must spend so much time fighting them(Bradley Is a Killer). According to the Hicks, the degens are egg huckers, nose pickers, and mailbox ruiners known for

characters.name: Degens
characters.description:
The DegensPortrayalFirst appearanceSuper Soft Birthday:For the Season 11 episode titled "Degens," seeDegens (episode).
TheDegens from Upcountry(short for "degenerates") are an unorganized group of louts who form the Hicks' primary antagonist onLetterkennythrough Season 4. One or more of their number scrap with the Hicks on numerous occasions. The termsdegenordegenerateare used to label anyone guilty of bad behavior, on various occasions. Katy is the first to use the term, of various individuals coming to thefarmto challengeWayne's title as Toughest Guy in Letterkenny(Super Soft Birthday): Look, keep it at the end of the laneway. No degens on the property.— Katy The Hicks ascribe all manner of bad behavior to the degens, and resent that they must spend so much time fighting them(Bradley Is a Killer). According to the Hicks, the degens are egg huckers, nose pickers, and mailbox ruiners known for

characters.name: Devon
characters.description: DevonAffiliationThe SkidsBiographical informationMarital statusSinglePhysical descriptionGenderMaleEyesBlueHairDark with a single blue highlightHeight6’0”PortrayalActorAlexander De JordyFirst appearanceAin't No Reason to Get ExcitedLast appearanceSt. Perfect's DayDevonis a supporting character onLetterkennyinSeasons 1and2. He is played byAlexander De Jordy. Devon is one ofthe Skids, the group of goth/emo outcasts led byStewartwhose lives center on drugs, video games, and music, and who spend their time in Stewart'sbasementor in the parking lot of thedollar store. Devon is a childhood friend of Stewart; they went halfsies on a receiver in grade six, and in grade three, Stewart had stolen a television from Devon's grandma to playEcco the Dolphinon Sega CD(Sled Shack). Devon emerges as the group's de facto second-in-command. He is the media wizard of the group, being the force behind the creation and marketing of Fartbook(Fartbook), and later McMurray's campaign video(The Election). He games, dances, and makes and does drugs with the Skids enthusiastically, but hates their petty vandalism, calling it "two-bit"(Sled Shack).

characters.name: Dierks
characters.description: Dierks Rensburg van KoeverdanDierks, moments before tilly timeAlso known asLong-Dick DierksRelativesElmo-Adrianus Rensburg-van Koeverdan(Cousin)Aldert-Kristoffel Rensburg-van Koeverdan(Cousin)Vas-Bram-Ambroos Rensburg-van Koeverdan(Cousin)Biographical informationMarital statusSinglePhysical descriptionGenderMalePortrayalAppearancesSeasons 8-10First appearance"The Rippers"Last appearance"King of Suckers” Dierksis a recurring character inSeason 8,Season 9, andSeason 10ofLetterkenny. He is played by Tyler Hynes. Dierks, also known as "Long-Dick Dierks" (and whose apparent last name is revealed in Season 10 to be "Rensburg-van-Koeverdan"), resides in Michigan across the border from Canada. He is a friend ofWayne's American cousinJakeand was also apparently acquainted withMcMurrayprior to being introduced on the show. Wayne and the otherHicksare introduced to Dierks at the "Snappers" strip club, where he had helped Jake plan Wayne's aborted bachelor party when he was engaged toMarie-Fred. He almost immediately gets under Wayne's skin with his excessively charming and phony "cool" demeanor, as well as his questions about Wayne's personal preferences in women and openness about his own turn-ons. Wayne avoids getting into a fight in the hopes that he'd never see Dierks again, but fate had other plans whenKaty, who matches Dierks' description of the ideal woman, enters the club and locks eyes with Dierks in a moment of instant chemistry.(The Rippers)

characters.name: Dolo
characters.description: DoloPhysical descriptionGenderMaleEyesbrownHairblackPortrayalActorJonathan DiabyFirst appearanceVeteran PresenceDolois a main character onShoresy, a defenseman for theSudbury Bulldogs. He is a fictionalized version of Canadian actor and former hockey playerJonathan Diaby, who performs as hip-hop artist JoDolo. Although Dolo understands English, and his songs are in English, on the show he only ever speaks in French. He refers to Shoresy asbonhomme, which can be variously translated depending on context as "good man," "good buddy," or "macho man," but is subtitled in the show as "handsome man." A native of Blainville, Quebec, Dolo was a former 3rd round pick in the NHL. His hockey career was cut short following an incident where a spectator taunted him with racial slurs and taunts and he responded by trying to fight. Since then, he has focused on his music; a clip of the music video for "Feeling Blessed" served as the cold open forVeteran Presence, and in lieu of a duotangShoresyplayed a clip from the video for "No Discussion" toNatwhen introducing the team's new players.

characters.name: Emma
characters.description: EmmaAlso known asBroDude RepAffiliationBroDude EnergyPhysical descriptionGenderFemaleEyesBrownHairBrownPortrayalActorVanessa MatsuiFirst appearanceThe RippersLast appearanceInternational Women's DayEmmais a recurring character onLetterkennyandShoresy, a marketing representative forBroDudeEnergy Drink. She is portrayed by Vanessa Matsui. Unnamed at first, she is first seen reluctantly making her way withCassie,Tassie, and a photographer throughMoDean's, where theKerry County Eagleshave been celebrating their victory in theNational Senior Hockey Championshipfor days. She findsReillyandJonesypassed out on the floor shirtless, cradling the cup, and tells them they have been selected as BroDude "brand ambassadors" — not that she had a say in the matter. For the record, no part of this, not a shard of it, not a ribbon of it, not a suggestion of it was my idea. Also, for the record, every fiber of my being, internal and external feels this is a horrifying waste of time, money, resources, and materials.— Emma

characters.name: Gae
characters.description: GaeAffiliationThe SkidsBiographical informationMarital statusSinglePhysical descriptionGenderFemaleEyesBlueHairDirty blondePortrayalActorSarah GadonFirst appearanceSled ShackGaeis a main character onLetterkennyinseasons 3and6, and appears as a guest inSeason 5. She is played bySarah Gadon. StewartinformedRoaldand the otherSkidsthatDevonhad disappeared, and proceeded to destroy things Devon had left behind in thebasement. Amidst this, someone costumed in a clown mask and rainbow clown costume threw stink bombs into the basement(Sled Shack). The figure later returned, but this time, the Skids were ready, and they accosted the intruder, whom they assumed at first to be Devon. To their shock, however, it was a girl(Puck Bunny). After some confusion about her name—initially, they interpreted "Gae" to refer to her sexual orientation—she explained that her parents banished her to live with her aunt in Letterkenny for poor behavior(MoDeans 2). She had chosen the clown costume for making mischief as clowns had been inflicting terror on the public all over North America. Gae gave three reasons for her rebelliousness: "I hate the world, I hate my parents, I hate myself." Her goal was to misbehave so much in Letterkenny that her parents would be forced to bring her back to the city. They organized the Skids into a group calling itself Freaks Acting Krayzee United (F.A.K.U., pronounced "fuck you"). F.A.K.U. then began a campaign of petty vandalism all over Letterkenny(MoDeans 2).

characters.name: Gail
characters.description: GailAlso known asGailerAffiliationModean'sRelativesBradley (cousin), Rosie (cousin)Physical descriptionGenderfemaleEyesbrownHairblackPortrayalActorLisa CodringtonFirst appearanceAin't No Reason to Get ExcitedLast appearanceSundays are for Picking StonesGailis a main character onLetterkenny. She is played byLisa Codrington. Gail is the owner and bartender atMoDean'sand its later incarnations, and the employer ofAlexanderand laterBonnie McMurrayandGlen. She is friends withthe Hicksand on good terms with most everyone in town. Little is known about her background or family, except that she is cousins withBradley—and his second favourite cousin(Bradley Is a Killer)—and withRosie, his first. Gail is oversexed, frequently talking about sex and attraction, in exaggerated tones and with obscene gestures. She had sex with Wayne and Katy's Uncle Eddie when he was in his 90s(The Haunting of MoDean's II), and at Wayne's Christmas party, did an erotic dance with a portrait of him(The Three Wise Men). When Uncle Eddie left them $5,000, she proposed spending it on a matchmaking scheme for Wayne similar toThe Bachelor, with a particular interest in his having sex with the three finalists(Uncle Eddie's Trust). For the Letterkenny Talent Show, she performed a monologue fromGlad He Ate Her XXX, a pornographic film cited as her favorite movie(Letterkenny Talent Show).

characters.name: The Ginger and Boots
characters.description: The Ginger and BootsPortrayalActor(uncredited)The GingerandBootsare minor characters onLetterkenny, primarily referenced as part of an extended running gag. The individuals portraying them are friends ofJared Keesofrom Listowel (Bell Media), and are uncredited, although Boots is known to be Keeso's former junior hockey teammate andLetterkenny Problemswriter Jordan Beirnes. Boots and the Ginger are known to be powerful fighters, and whenWayneis faced with the prospect of fighting bothJoint Boyand his entourage, Daryl suggests that they be brought in for backup(Super Soft Birthday). He has not kept up with the town gossip, however. Wayne: Yeah, but… well like, you heard he fucked an ostrich, right?Daryl: He what?Wayne: He fucked an ostrich?Dan:Allegedly.

characters.name: Glen
characters.description: GlenAlso known asPastor GlenBiographical informationMarital statusSingleVehicleScooter, BicyclePhysical descriptionGenderMaleEyesBlueHairBrownPortrayalActorJacob TierneyFirst appearanceAin't No Reason to Get ExcitedLast appearanceOver and Out Glenis a main character onLetterkenny, played byJacob Tierney. In addition to playing Glen, Jacob Tierney is also a co-creator, writer, and director ofLetterkenny. At the start of the series, Glen is an evangelical Christian preacher, pastor of theGrace Fellowship Church(Rave)and director of the Burning Bush Youth Ministry(Super Soft Birthday). The show hints that he is gay on a number of occasions (most notably, in(In It To Win It)), with an ongoing joke being his comically over the top flamboyant mannerisms, religious sermons with underlying sexual overtones directed towards men & his frequently eating popsicles, suckers and other similar shaped foods in a very suggestable manner. Despite this  his potential overt homosexuality is never outright mentioned. Glen's accent suggests he is originally from the American South, but his origin or early biography has not been explored.
